source_pr.title: planner, executor, statistics: add tests for version 2 and fix bugs
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

This pull adds tests for the following cases:
- Analyze when changing the session variables
- The behavior of auto analyze in background.
- Fast analyze/Incremental analyze(note that this two are not GA currently)

### What is changed and how it works?

Behavior of changing the session variables:
- If it's an analyze table, following the session's.
- If it's an analyze index and there's existing stats on that table, use the existing one.

Behavior of auto analyze:
- If this table already has stats, use the existing analyze version of it.
- If not, following the global variable.

How auto analyze works:
- A background work updates the session var of the handle, making it updated to the global one.
- Then checking whether this table is analyzed or not, to choose which analyze version to be sent.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test

Side effects

- Performance regression
    - Consumes more CPU
    - Consumes more MEM
- Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- No release note

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to concurrency races or unsynchronized shared state. Asynchronous state transitions need polling/Eventually-style checks. This PR stabilizes it by: use lock to avoid race.

evidence: evidence: use lock to avoid race
